title: Overrides of Dispose(bool) should call the base implementation
messageFormat: Call 'base.Dispose(disposing)' so the base type can release its own resources.
summary: The dispose pattern relies on every level of an inheritance chain releasing the resources it owns. When a type overrides Dispose(bool) but never invokes the base version, the resources held by base types are silently leaked, and the leak only surfaces under load when handles or memory run out.
An override of Dispose(bool) should forward the call to base.Dispose(disposing) on every path so the chain stays intact.
